response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with 20+ years of experience, but his profile is almost entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and does not match this role's core requirements. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no L2/L3 networking or switch background, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal). C experience is limited to a brief stint over 15 years ago, far short of the strong UNIX C proficiency required.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop and some embedded work
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
Some low-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and DLang work on robotic arms and low-latency systems
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability, which is a mandatory native-level requirement
No experience with L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec and IEEE 802.1X
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel analysis or source-modification experience

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Insufficient C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
